text: Risk management is an important process for any project or business. It begins with identifying risks, which means recognizing potential problems that could cause delays or failures. Once risks are identified, teams create mitigation strategies to reduce their impact or likelihood, such as improving safety procedures or diversifying suppliers. However, not all risks can be prevented, so contingency plans are also made. These are backup actions to take if a risk actually occurs, like having extra funds or spare equipment ready. Finally, risk management involves continuous monitoring. Teams regularly review risks and plans, updating them as situations change, ensuring the project stays on track.
